data.title: Data centers turn to jet engines and diesel generators as AI power needs surge
data.description:
Companies building data centers are increasingly relying on jet engine-based turbines and fossil fuel generators to meet electricity demands, as bottlenecks in supply chains and lengthy grid connection processes push developers toward alternative power solutions. Producers of turbines derived from aircraft engines and diesel-powered generators say orders have jumped as data center operators look for […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/data-centers-jet-engines-diesel-generators/

data.title: Bitmain Reduces Bitcoin Mining Rig Prices Amid Market Pressure
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bitmain has significantly lowered the prices of its Bitcoin mining rigs, with several models from the S19 and S21 series now available at reduced rates. Internal pricing reveals that the S19e XP Hydro and 3U S19 XP Hydro are priced as low as $3 per TH/s, while the S19 XP+ Hydro is approximately $4 per TH/s. The S21 immersion machine is around $7 per TH/s, and the S21+ Hydro is about $8 per TH/s. Previously, the company offered a bundled package for the S19 XP+ Hydro, priced at roughly $4 per TH/s, and in November, initiated auction-style sales for the S19k Pro starting at $5.5 per TH/s.

Analysts have noted that the network's hash rate remains near historical highs, while Bitcoin's price has declined, resulting in hash rate prices hovering near multi-year lows. This situation has increased pressure on the mining industry, squeezing miners' profit margins and reducing demand for new equipment, particularly less efficient models. Additionally, it has intensified competition between ASIC mining rig manufacturers and sellers in the second-hand market.

data.title: KLab Initiates Dual Gold Financial Strategy with Bitcoin and Gold Purchases
data.description:
According to PANews, Japanese publicly traded company KLab has announced the commencement of its 'Dual Gold Financial Strategy,' involving the phased acquisition of Bitcoin and gold based on price fluctuations. The company plans to allocate 3.6 billion yen from its approximately 5.1 billion yen in raised funds, purchasing Bitcoin and gold in a 6:4 ratio. KLab describes the decline in yen purchasing power as a 'silent collapse' and has developed this new strategy to safeguard shareholder value against inflation and yen depreciation pressures.

As of December 25, KLab has increased its Bitcoin holdings to a total of 4.36828 BTC, having recently acquired 3.17 BTC at an average price of 13.83 million yen, in addition to its previously held 1.19828 BTC. The company has also invested in pure gold ETFs at a price of 21,514 yen.

data.title: Silver's Potential Target Price Analyzed by Veteran Trader
data.description: According to PANews, veteran trader Peter Brandt has stated that based on the quarterly average directional index (ADX), silver is far from being overbought. Brandt suggests that $147 is a reasonable target price for silver.

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Bull Market Predicted to Last Until 2035
data.description: According to PANews, Jan3 CEO Samson Mow recently stated on the X platform that while 2025 is expected to be a bear market, Bitcoin may soon enter a bull market that could extend until 2035.

data.title: Falling Miner Profitability Forces Bitmain to Reprice Bitcoin Mining Hardware
data.description:
The global market for Bitcoin mining equipment is showing clear signs of strain, with Bitmain cutting prices aggressively across much of its product lineup.
source: https://cryptodnes.bg/en/falling-miner-profitability-forces-bitmain-to-reprice-bitcoin-mining-hardware/

data.title: Solana Co-Founder Predicts Key Developments by 2026
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the co-founder of Solana shared insights on social media regarding anticipated advancements by 2026 in areas such as stablecoins, cutting-edge technology, and artificial intelligence. The predictions include the stablecoin market surpassing $1 trillion, while breakthroughs in quantum computing and controlled nuclear fusion remain elusive. Additionally, artificial intelligence is expected to solve a 'millennium mathematical problem,' and the shipment of humanoid robots is projected to reach 100,000 units.

data.title: Significant Outflows from U.S. Spot Bitcoin ETFs This Week
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Farside monitoring has reported a net outflow of $589.4 million from U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs this week. The breakdown of the outflows is as follows:

IBIT experienced a net outflow of $242.7 million, while FBTC saw $110.7 million withdrawn. BITB had a net outflow of $54 million, and ARKB recorded $31.3 million in outflows. EZBC faced a net outflow of $5.1 million, and HODL saw $41.6 million withdrawn. GBTC experienced a net outflow of $72.8 million, and Grayscale BTC had $31.2 million in outflows.

data.title: Strategy Bolsters Liquidity Management with $2.2 Billion Cash Reserve
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Strategy is intensifying its focus on liquidity management by establishing a $2.2 billion cash reserve. This move aims to facilitate the payment of preferred stock dividends and debt interest without the need to sell Bitcoin holdings.

The cash reserve is considered a crucial step in stabilizing the company's financial structure and mitigating the risk of forced asset sales, as the valuation premium relative to its Bitcoin holdings gradually declines.

According to reports, the creators claimed that the new tool could be used to modify others’ works without their consent. Reports claimed that the exodus is being led […]

data.title: Strategy Prepares Defensive Strategy Amid Bitcoin Challenges
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, a recent analysis reported by CNBC highlights that the stock price of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy remains low, with the Bitcoin premium indicator also declining. A crucial decision regarding whether the MSCI index will exclude Strategy is expected in January. In response to this situation, Strategy is adopting a 'defensive mode' strategy. Recently, the company has established a cash buffer of approximately $2.2 billion to withstand the challenges of its Bitcoin investments. This fund is anticipated to be used for paying preferred stock dividends and debt interest rather than purchasing additional Bitcoin.
